root_text.sn45.165:1.5: Imāsaṁ kho, bhikkhave, tissannaṁ dukkhatānaṁ abhiññāya pariññāya parikkhayāya pahānāya …pe… ayaṁ ariyo aṭṭhaṅgiko maggo bhāvetabbo”ti.

translation_text.sn45.165:1.1: “Mendicants, there are these three forms of suffering.
translation_text.sn45.165:1.3: The suffering inherent in painful feeling; the suffering inherent in conditions; and the suffering inherent in perishing.
translation_text.sn45.165:1.4: These are the three forms of suffering.
translation_text.sn45.165:1.5: The noble eightfold path should be developed for the direct knowledge, complete understanding, finishing, and giving up of these three forms of suffering.”
